title: dendrophthoe chrysantha
intro: Dendrophthoe chrysantha, also known as the gold orchid vine or yellow mistletoe, is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, obtaining nutrients by connecting to the host's vascular system. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits associated with Dendrophthoe chrysantha include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by decocting the leaves and stems in water, and the resulting extract can be used to treat various ailments.
